pr.title: Match synth and Ice Mountain functions
pr.author: itsgrimetime

summary: Matched one sysdolphin synth function and three Ice Mountain stage functions. The PR removed the stale residual-match note for HSD_Synth_80388DC8, tightened the HSD_Synth_8038B120 header prototype to void(void), and reshaped grIceMt_801F96E0, grIceMt_801F993C, and grIceMt_801FA0BC to reach 100.0% in the GALE01 report.

key_files.role: Main Ice Mountain matching work for grIceMt_801F96E0, grIceMt_801F993C, and grIceMt_801FA0BC.
Removed obsolete @todo note after HSD_Synth_80388DC8 reached 100%.
Changed HSD_Synth_8038B120 prototype from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to void(void).

what_changed: Added '#pragma auto_inline off' before grIceMt_801F71E8 in gricemt.c.
Changed several Ice Mountain HSD_ASSERT/HSD_ASSERTMSG macro uses into explicit __assert calls with grIm_803E4068 string-table offsets and literal assertion names/messages.
In grIceMt_801F993C, expanded upper_ix and under_ix range assertions into explicit conditional __assert calls using message pointers at grIm_803E4068 offsets 0x828 and 0x844.

Reworked grIceMt_801FA0BC: removed the previous grIceMt_801F71E8_inner/grIceMt_801F71E8_noinline wrappers, introduced grIceMt_FA0BC_jobjs(Ground*) returning &g->gv.icemt2.xC8, added PAD_STACK(24), renamed/retyped locals, and used direct grIceMt_801F71E8 calls.
In grIceMt_801FA0BC, replaced -20.0f * Ground_801C0498() source form with grIm_804DB570 * Ground_801C0498() to match the existing data constant usage.
In grIceMt_801FA0BC, made NULL checks explicit before __assert and changed pointer-array handling to a scoped HSD_JObj** jobjs local with explicit jobjs[n] != NULL tests.
Removed the obsolete comment saying HSD_Synth_80388DC8 was only a 97.2% match.
Updated synth.h so HSD_Synth_8038B120 is declared as void HSD_Synth_8038B120(void) instead of UNK_RET HSD_Synth_8038B120(UNK_PARAMS).
smart_moves: Used explicit __assert calls where assertion macros did not produce the desired source shape, preserving exact file/message pointer behavior needed for matching.
Replaced an artificial noinline-call wrapper pattern with a cleaner direct-call shape plus auto-inline control.
Introduced a tiny static inline accessor, grIceMt_FA0BC_jobjs, to influence codegen for the HSD_JObj** array without changing the underlying structure access.
Used PAD_STACK(24) as a targeted stack-frame matching tactic in grIceMt_801FA0BC.
Tightened an unknown synth prototype only after evidence from the existing definition and successful --require-protos verification.
Cleaned up stale match-status documentation in synth.c once the GALE01 report showed HSD_Synth_80388DC8 at 100.0%.
decomp_lessons: HSD_ASSERT and HSD_ASSERTMSG are not always source-shape neutral; expanding them into __assert can be necessary when matching exact string pointers, line numbers, or register allocation.
For stage code that indexes data tables such as grIm_803E4068, matching may require referencing known string/data offsets instead of relying on macro-generated literals.
Small accessor helpers that return a pointer to a struct subfield can be useful to reproduce pointer-array setup and aliasing behavior.
Using named data constants, such as grIm_804DB570, can match generated code better than spelling the same value as a float literal.
PAD_STACK remains a practical tactic when the compiler needs an otherwise-unused stack reservation to match the original frame.
Once a function is matched, remove stale residual-match notes and replace UNK prototypes with concrete signatures when verified by configure.py --require-protos.
naming_conventions: Ice Mountain stage functions use the grIceMt_801Fxxxx address-based prefix, for example grIceMt_801F96E0, grIceMt_801F993C, and grIceMt_801FA0BC.
The new local helper name grIceMt_FA0BC_jobjs ties the helper to the target function suffix and the HSD_JObj** role.
Ice Mountain file-scope data symbols in this slice use the grIm_ prefix, including grIm_803E4068, grIm_804D69F4, and grIm_804DB570.
Sysdolphin synth symbols retain HSD_Synth/HSD_SynthSFX prefixes, such as HSD_Synth_80388DC8 and HSD_SynthSFXUnloadBank.
Unknown generic prototypes should be tightened from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to concrete signatures when the definition is known and require-protos passes.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: #pragma auto_inline off near grIceMt_801F71E8 to control inlining behavior.
Explicit __assert((char*) &grIm_803E4068 + offset, line, message) calls to reproduce assert call operands.
PAD_STACK(24) in grIceMt_801FA0BC to match stack layout.
Use of a static inline helper returning &g->gv.icemt2.xC8 to shape pointer computation for jobjs.
Scoped block around HSD_JObj** jobjs and explicit NULL comparisons to shape generated control flow.
Use of grIm_804DB570 instead of a direct -20.0f literal for translate-Y calculations.
Verification commands reported by the PR: python configure.py --require-protos and ninja.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided slice.
The PR body/comment reported GALE01 100.0% results for HSD_Synth_80388DC8, grIceMt_801F96E0, grIceMt_801F993C, and grIceMt_801FA0BC.
searchable_terms: Match synth and Ice Mountain functions
follow_up_queries: Find other remaining HSD_ASSERT/HSD_ASSERTMSG nonmatches in gricemt.c that may need explicit __assert expansion.
Search for stale @todo residual-match comments in sysdolphin/baselib after GALE01 reports reach 100%.
Audit UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS prototypes in synth.h against existing function definitions under --require-protos.
Look for other Ice Mountain helpers where small static inline accessors or PAD_STACK could improve matching.
